steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to "provide a specific example of onomatopoeia." A specific example means naming an actual onomatopoeia word and showing how it imitates a sound — not defining the term, explaining variation in general, or noting that different languages use different words.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The term "onomatopoeia," which refers to words that imitate the sound they represent, combines the Greek words for "name" and "to make.": defines the term's etymology. No specific onomatopoeia word is named or demonstrated. ✗
  • Choice B — The Russian word "tuk-tuk" both imitates and represents the sound of a heartbeat.: names the specific word "tuk-tuk," identifies it as Russian, and shows exactly what sound it imitates. This is a concrete example. ✓
  • Choice C — Differences in how languages are structured can cause variations in the words used in different languages to represent the same sound.: explains variation in general terms. No specific onomatopoeia word appears. ✗
  • Choice D — The languages of Korean and Russian use different words to represent the sound of a heartbeat.: acknowledges that different words exist but never names any of them. Doesn't provide a specific example. ✗
That is the correct answer.
